title: Chief Technology Officer
org: Buddimm - Real Estate Consulting Startup (~10 people)
description: Joined as a software engineering intern; hired as CTO after the internship to own the company's entire technical operation.
achievements: Built and run the company's full technical infrastructure: public website, internal tools, and the lead-generation pipeline
Rolled out Claude for Teams company-wide and designed ~20 custom AI skills and connectors automating recurring workflows
Defined which processes to automate with the CEO, cutting time wasted on repetitive tasks
Trained the team on effective AI usage, good and bad use cases, prompting, and process integration

title: Freelance Consultant
description: Modernized the optimization algorithm of a production-line machine, software that computes the optimal output parameters of each component to maximize the line's working speed.
achievements: Translated a 25,000+ line algorithm from CODESYS Structured Text to modern C++
Worked without access to the closed-source compiler or a way to run the original scripts, validated behavior entirely through systematic manual debugging
Re-optimized the ported algorithm and extended it with new complex features beyond the original scope

title: Freelance Consultant
description: Independent security assessment of a live AI SaaS platform. Covered the full stack, web application, APIs, database access controls, billing integration, and AI chat layer.
achievements: Mapped the production attack surface through full-stack reconnaissance without relying on internal documentation
Ran authorized, non-destructive penetration testing on production with live proof-of-concept validation
Performed LLM-specific security analysis across the AI chat pipeline (prompt injection, tool abuse, data integrity)
Delivered a structured report with prioritized remediation roadmap and post-fix verification guidance

title: Research Intern - Behavioral Changes Caused by Virtual Reality
org: Suwa University of Science, Nagano, Japan
description: Two-month research internship on behavioral changes caused by Virtual Reality, working with Professor Yamaguchi Takehiko.
achievements: Designed and executed VR experiments to study user behavior and psychological impacts of immersive technologies
Analyzed experimental data using statistical methods, identifying key behavioral patterns and correlations
Developed VR prototypes using Unity for research simulations and user testing
Presented research findings at university symposiums

title: Research Intern - CAD & Fabrication of Carbon Fiber Aircraft
description: Participated in the 2024 NPU Sino-Euro Spring School at NPU, a leading aerospace engineering university. Worked with Professor Lubin Huo on carbon fiber applications in the aircraft industry.
achievements: Leveraged computer tools for designing and manufacturing carbon fiber-reinforced composite laminated aircraft crafts
Studied advanced techniques for computer-aided design and fabrication of composite materials
Explored applications of carbon fiber composites in improving aircraft performance and fuel efficiency
Participated in hands-on sessions and research discussions with leading aerospace engineering professors

title: Competitive Programming
description: Competing in international and national computer science competitions since 2020.
achievements: 2nd in France, 40th in Europe at the 2026 ICPC European Championship
3rd in France at ICPC SWERC 2025, qualified for the European Championship
Competing again at SWERC 2026
Part of the INSA Lyon ICPC team (2024, 2025)
4-time finalist in Prologin (2022, 2023, 2024, 2025)
1st place in a France-IOI contest (Castor Informatique 2020) among 500,000+ participants
1st place in Match'Up Coding Battle 2020 among 2,000+ participants
Designing contest exercises for INSAlgo and Le Match'Up

title: Computer Science & Engineering - Informatique (IF)
org: INSA Lyon - LyonTech La Doua, Villeurbanne
description: Five-year engineering curriculum at one of France's leading public grandes écoles, specializing in the Informatique (IF) department, a reference CS track training multi-skilled engineers across algorithms, software, systems, networks, and AI.
achievements: Three-year IF specialization after the common first cycle, fundamentals, team-based projects, and electives, with yearly industry internships growing in scope
Advanced coursework in algorithms, systems, networks, and software integration, reinforced through ICPC-level competitive programming practice
Project-based learning culture aligned with real-world engineering: data structures, large-scale software design, and cross-disciplinary team projects

title: Community Leader - INSAlgo & Tech Associations
